items.headline: The Clarity Act won’t lead to adoption without crypto tax reform
items.subheadline: The legislation is being framed as a turning point for U.S. crypto policy, but there’s a major piece of the puzzle being overlooked, argues Singh.
items.author: Robin Singh

items.headline: A bipartisan bridge to the future: Why the Senate must finish the job on digital Assets
items.subheadline: The Clarity Act’s recent markup proved that the momentum for regulation is there. It is imperative that Congress move the bill forward to establish rules this generation needs and a framework the next will inherit, urges Kim.
items.author: Ji Hun Kim

items.headline: Disciplined AI agents are the disruptor needed to break the exchange churn model
items.subheadline: Programmable incentives that allow independent trading agents to earn only when portfolios rise will create a fairer market for retail customers, explains Naja.
items.author: Saad Naja

items.headline: What American crypto asset perpetuals mean for the future of crypto
items.subheadline: For years, one of the most significant crypto asset markets has existed entirely outside the United States. Today, that changes, explains CFTC Chairman Selig.
items.author: Michael S. Selig

items.headline: Bitcoin (BTC) underperforms risk assets as record 9th day of ETF outflows signal waning demand: Crypto Daily
items.subheadline: The day ahead in crypto: May 29, 2026
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: ICE CEO calls Hyperliquid bigger than NASDAQ, says he's met its founders
items.subheadline: Sprecher saluted Hyperliquid's team, signaling deeper engagement between Wall Street incumbents and crypto-native rails.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Paxos wins SEC approval to clear U.S. stocks on blockchain
items.subheadline: Paxos new license positions it alongside legacy giants such as DTCC and makes it a compelling, more efficient alternative for traditional finance giants than legacy competitors.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: CoinDesk 20 performance update: Bittensor (TAO) drops 4%, leading index lower
items.subheadline: Internet Computer (ICP), down 3.8%, was also an underperformer.
items.author: CoinDesk Indices

items.headline: U.S. CFTC opens crypto 'perp' door with first approval at regulated firm
items.subheadline: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has now established a stance on how regulated U.S. firms can engage in crypto perpetual futures contracts.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Crypto PACs spend $9 million in Texas and score wins in both parties
items.subheadline: Industry-backed PACs supported Democratic and Republican candidates alike, underscoring digital assets’ growing role as a cross-party electoral force.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: The government should promote innovation, not punish it
items.subheadline: District Attorney of Sacramento County, Ho argues that ambiguous rules and enforcement practices, such as those for crypto, lead to confusion, rather than seeking clarity in the law to further growth.
items.author: Thien Ho

items.headline: XRP news: Ripple-linked blockchain could close its biggest DeFi gap if new proposal passes
items.subheadline: A draft proposal filed Tuesday would extend the XRP Ledger's native automated market maker with three swappable curve types, giving liquidity providers more efficient ways to deploy capital and closing one of XRPL DeFi's longest-standing gaps.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Hyperliquid's pre-IPO SpaceX contracts suffer 45% flash crash, liquidating $1.5 million
items.subheadline: A massive selloff in a SpaceX crypto token wiped out hundreds of retail traders in 30 minutes because the market lacked enough cash to absorb the shock.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: Ethereum news: Bit Digital (BTBT) bought ether first time since October before 15% decline
items.subheadline: The firm said it purchased 8,568 ether on May 11 at an average price of $2,334. Since then, ETH broke below $2,000, leaving the firm sitting at a $3 million unrealized loss.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: VanEck launches first U.S. spot BNB ETF on Nasdaq
items.subheadline: The ETF offers investors BNB exposure through traditional brokerage accounts without requiring them to directly buy or store the token.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Grayscale IPO delayed as crypto firms reassess public market plans
items.subheadline: Grayscale’s decision to delay its IPO highlights the growing caution across the crypto industry as volatile markets and weak investor demand weigh on public listing plans.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Toddlers learn by falling: Why DeFi's $20 billion TVL drop is just a market stress-test
items.subheadline: DeFi Technologies president Andrew Forson says the stablecoin layer is thriving, with more than $150 billion in U.S. Treasuries backing coins like USDT and USDC.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: UniCredit warns Europe may struggle to contain crypto-bank crisis under MiCA rules
items.subheadline: EU deposit insurance (up to €100,000) may not absorb stress from large stablecoin reserve accounts, unlike the full protection offered by U.S. regulators.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Tether's U.S.-focused stablecoin grows 500% in a month, but still lags Circle, Ripple, Paypal
items.subheadline: USAT's market cap jumped above $140 million in April, though it remains well behind Circle's USDC, PayPal's PYUSD and Ripple's RLUSD tokens.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Crypto trading firm FalconX confidentially files with SEC for IPO, hires bankers
items.subheadline: FalconX has hired Cantor and other bankers to advise on a potential IPO and confidentially filed draft paperwork with the SEC, though the listing is not expected until later this year amid volatile market conditions.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Bitcoin's record holder supply hides a buyer drought, CryptoQuant says
items.subheadline: A record high in long-term holder supply typically signals conviction. CryptoQuant says it reflects a shortage of new buyers, a view echoed by weakening ETF demand and bearish prediction market odds.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: BTC, ETH price news: Bitcoin, ether steady despite record stocks, falling oil and easing war fears
items.subheadline: Global stocks hit records and oil cracked on a tentative US-Iran ceasefire extension. Crypto stayed on the sidelines, with some analysts saying the next catalyst is regulatory, not geopolitical.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: XRP rebounds above $1.30 after volume surge, but bears still control the bigger picture
items.subheadline: XRP snapped a streak of lower lows on heavy buying, though the token remains stuck below major resistance levels that have capped every rally this year.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: OKX Ventures buys $53 million stake in Korea's Coinone exchange
items.subheadline: The crypto exchange's investment arm and Korea Investment & Securities will each invest KRW 80 billion ($53 million) as Coinone pushes into stablecoins and tokenized securities.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: STRC slips below par as Strategy's (MSTR) cash reserves face growing scrutiny
items.subheadline: STRC has struggled to maintain its $100 target price, while Strategy's reduced cash reserves and dividend obligations are drawing increased investor attention.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Crypto and stocks go their separate ways as bitcoin's failed breakout continues to weigh
items.subheadline: Bitcoin's failed breakout above $83,000 is looking increasingly like a bear market signal, even as S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures notch gains and approach all-time highs.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: Bitcoin ETFs suffer record 9-day outflow streak as $2.8 billion exits funds
items.subheadline: The longest run of withdrawals since U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs listed in January 2024 comes as bitcoin underperforms high-flying AI and semiconductor stocks.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Coinbase (COIN) news: Base launches AI tool that lets ChatGPT manage crypto wallets and DeFi apps
items.subheadline: The product, called Base MCP, connects a user’s Base Account to AI clients such as ChatGPT, Claude and Cursor using the Model Context Protocol (MCP), an emerging standard that allows AI systems to securely interface with external tools and applications.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: DeFi isn't safe anymore because AI is becoming 'superhuman' at hacking, onetime OpenZeppelin founder says
items.subheadline: As hacks pile up and DeFi TVL falls fast, OpenZeppelin co-founder and former CTO Manuel Aráoz says AI coding agents have made smart contracts fatally vulnerable.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: Kraken debuts Bitcoin Vault as demand grows for BTC yield products
items.subheadline: The new earn product lets users generate BTC-denominated rewards through DeFi strategies while keeping exposure to bitcoin’s price.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Gemini (GEMI) taps SpaceXAI to build a personalized prediction markets feed
items.subheadline: The new feature integrates SpaceXAI models to deliver real-time market intelligence, personalized signals and portfolio insights directly inside the Gemini app.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: A blockchain lottery plans to use crypto gambling fees to fund Ethereum development
items.subheadline: Decentralized lottery protocol Megapot is teaming up with Protocol Guild to launch what the firms describe as the crypto industry’s first programmable charity lottery.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Sui blockchain suffers another network outage as transactions grind to a halt
items.subheadline: This is the second time this year that the Sui network has suffered an outage.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Why the Ethereum Foundation is suddenly again at the center of crypto’s culture war
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of the Protocol Newsletter, we're diving deep into the institution that has been the main steward for the Ethereum blockchain, and why its been back in the spotlight.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Solana, Sui and Aptos wallet data targeted in TrapDoor package attack
items.subheadline: The campaign targets crypto, DeFi, AI and security developers with fake tooling packages to steal wallets, SSH keys, GitHub tokens, cloud credentials and browser data.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: The legal risks and practical considerations of digital asset blacklisting
items.subheadline: When digital assets are frozen, holders can suddenly be deprived of access to their legitimate assets or income. Here’s what to know to keep your digital holdings safe.
items.author: Galen Kast

items.headline: The time is now: the Senate must act on crypto market structure legislation
items.subheadline: The United States needs to finally establish the clear framework that the market needs.
items.author: Summer Mersinger

items.headline: How DeFi is changing the financial landscape for Latin Americans
items.subheadline: DeFi is quietly shifting from niche crypto experiment to a legitimate financial tool across the region, explains Serrano.
items.author: Sebastián Serrano

items.headline: It’s time for clarity for America’s digital asset markets
items.subheadline: Recent polls have shown that registered voters want America to set the rules for global finance, reinforcing why the Senate must act now, explains Smith.
items.author: Kristin Smith

items.headline: Iran war shows markets no longer sleep
items.subheadline: The latest conflict involving Iran has produced an unexpected proving ground for financial infrastructure, and an unlikely winner has emerged, argues Huang.
items.author: Annabelle Huang

items.headline: The U.S. can’t lose the bitcoin race to China
items.subheadline: The next global power competition is not being fought over missiles alone. It’s being fought over money, and right now, China is moving aggressively to shape the future of it, argues Gooden.
items.author: Congressman Lance Gooden

items.headline: The agentic CFO in your pocket
items.subheadline: Chalom explains that retail investors have never had the opportunity to access and manage their own digital treasury desk. Until now.
items.author: Joseph Chalom

items.headline: Singapore charges former Hodlnaut CEO Zhu Juntao over Terra collapse claims
items.subheadline: Zhu allegedly directed staff to publish false claims that Hodlnaut had no direct exposure to the TerraUSD collapse on Telegram and in customer emails.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Wall Street gets new crypto rival after Texas bank completes regulatory pivot
items.subheadline: By establishing a national charter under the executive branch, United Texas Bank said it intends to enable AI-driven payment rails to intercept global digital dollar volumes.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Google engineer insider-traded search results on Polymarket, Feds allege
items.subheadline: The charges mark the second major arrest over insider trading on a prediction market.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: U.S. CFTC files request to erase Gemini settlement that it no longer considers fair
items.subheadline: The regulator went back to analyze the 2022 case and decided that it wouldn't have been pursued under current management and practices.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: White House reviews CFTC prediction-market rule as Trump backs federal control
items.subheadline: The proposal could shape how Kalshi, Polymarket, and other event-contract platforms operate as states challenge the CFTC’s authority.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: Kalshi follows CFTC in suing Minnesota over its law criminalizing prediction markets
items.subheadline: The Governor of Minnesota signed into law a measure that starting August 1 makes it a crime to advertise and operate prediction market platforms across the state.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Bitcoin price news: BTC off the lows on Trump post, but set to close month of May with losses
items.subheadline: A hopeful posting on Iran from President Trump helped erase morning losses.
items.author: Stephen Alpher

items.headline: Clarity Act Risks Regulation Without Oversight, Brookings Fellow Says
items.subheadline: As Congress weighs crypto legislation, Aaron Klein says the CFTC needs more resources, independence and coordination to oversee digital markets.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: Mass deployment of AI agents is a disaster waiting to happen, says CertiK CEO
items.subheadline: Ronghui Gu shares tips on how to isolate AI agents while testing them so they do not have access to critical personal information or digital assets.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: U.S. regulator says 24/7 trading is great for crypto, may not be fit for other sectors
items.subheadline: As the CFTC issued landmark approvals for crypto perpetual futures contracts, it explained in a related advisory that round-the-clock activity isn't right for all.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: ‘The banks will not accept it’: JP Morgan's Dimon escalates battle over stablecoin rewards in CLARITY Act debate
items.subheadline: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon criticized Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong and warned the current CLARITY Act framework could ultimately fail, as banks and crypto firms clash over whether stablecoin issuers should be allowed to offer yield-bearing rewards that resemble bank deposits.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Bitcoin’s biggest quantum risk may not be wallet keys. An early investor fears something bigger
items.subheadline: Andrew Gault, the venture capitalist who funded the quantum hardware labs now threatening bitcoin, says the industry is looking in the wrong place. Google's own security team moved in the same direction in March.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bitcoin, ether, XRP, dogecoin lag a nine-week stocks rally as ETF demand cools
items.subheadline: The S&P 500 posted its longest weekly winning streak since 2023 and Brent oil stabilized near $92 on US-Iran ceasefire hopes. The biggest cryptocurrencies still drifted lower, with Hyperliquid's HYPE the only major name to rally.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: XRP news: Ripple-linked ETFs drew inflows last week as bitcoin, ether funds lost $2 billion
items.subheadline: From May 20 to May 29, XRP funds took in $35 million while bitcoin and ether ETFs lost roughly $2 billion combined, with Ripple’s earlier reported XRP treasury plan still awaiting confirmation.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Grayscale says Hyperliquid could become a ‘financial services juggernaut’
items.subheadline: The digital asset manager says Hyperliquid is emerging as a blockchain-based financial infrastructure platform with the potential to expand beyond crypto trading and challenge parts of traditional derivatives and exchange markets.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Binance aims for 3 billion users by 2030 amid a market it says is going through hard times
items.subheadline: Established crypto firms will merge with traditional finance, but neither Wall Street bankers nor corporate giants will take over the crypto industry, said Binance’s Head of VIP and Institutional, Catherine Chen.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Wall Street’s trillion-dollar dilemma: Why AI-powered hackers are keeping big banks off the blockchain
items.subheadline: CertiK CEO and co-founder Ronghui Gu says April was the worst month for DeFi in four years with exploits on 27 out of 30 day
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: U.S. says it seized about $1 billion in Iranian crypto as pressure campaign expands
items.subheadline: The seizure, falling under "Operation Economic Fury," aims to restrict Iran's access to overseas revenue, banking networks, and crypto infrastructure.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: SEC sues Texas man over $12.3 million alleged crypto scheme built on fake AI trading bots
items.subheadline: Fuller allegedly diverted $6.2 million for personal use and $5.5M for Ponzi-like payments; only 3% of funds went to crypto trading.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: XRP Ledger's design blocks the flash loan attacks costing DeFi hundreds of millions
items.subheadline: A draft XRPL amendment notes that flash loan attacks are "structurally impossible" on the network because of how its transactions are built, an architectural quirk that has spared the chain from the exploit class that has cost Ethereum DeFi billions.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa
